title: Deno Deployment
description: Represents a Deno Deploy deployment, which is an immutable unit of code, static assets, environment variables, and configuration that runs on Deno Deploy edge infrastructure. Deployments are created from a set of assets and an entry point, and become active when attached to a domain or project subdomain. In the v2 API, deployments are called revisions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the deployment, assigned by Deno Deploy at creation time

projectId.description: UUID of the project that owns this deployment

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the deployment

domains.description: List of custom domain names currently serving traffic from this deployment

items.description: A fully qualified domain name attached to this deployment

envVars.description: Environment variables configured for this deployment. Secret variable values are redacted in API responses.

databases.description: Deno KV database associations for this deployment, keyed by the binding name used within the deployment code

additionalProperties.description: UUID of an associated Deno KV database

assets.description: Map of file paths to asset definitions included in this deployment

entryPointUrl.description: Relative path to the main entry point module within the assets, executed when the deployment receives a request

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the deployment was created

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the deployment was last modified

DeploymentAsset.description: An individual file asset included in a Deno Deploy deployment. Assets can be UTF-8 text files, base64-encoded binary files, references to previously uploaded git blob objects by SHA-1 hash, or symbolic links.

kind.description: The asset type, determining how the content field is interpreted

content.description: The file content as a UTF-8 string (for text files) or a base64-encoded string (for binary files). Not used for symlink assets.

encoding.description: The encoding of the content field for file assets

gitSha1.description: SHA-1 hash of a previously uploaded git blob object. When provided, the content and encoding fields are ignored and the blob content is used instead.

target.description: Target path for symlink assets, relative to the project root

CompilerOptions.description: TypeScript compiler options applied when building a Deno Deploy deployment

jsx.description: JSX transform mode controlling how JSX syntax is compiled

jsxFactory.description: The function called to create JSX elements (default: React.createElement)

jsxFragmentFactory.description: The function called to create JSX fragments (default: React.Fragment)

jsxImportSource.description: Module specifier used as the source for the JSX automatic runtime

BuildLogEntry.description: A single entry from a deployment's build log, capturing output from the compilation and bundling process

message.description: Log message content produced during the build

ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this build log entry was produced

AppLogEntry.description: A single runtime application log entry emitted by a running deployment. Entries are produced by console.log and related calls within the deployed code.

region.description: Deno Deploy region identifier where this log entry was produced (e.g., us-east-1)

deploymentId.description: Identifier of the deployment that produced this log entry

ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this log entry was produced

DeploymentEvent.description: A system-level event recorded in deployment logs. Events capture significant runtime lifecycle occurrences such as isolate boot, memory limit exceeded, or CPU time limit exceeded.

message.description: Human-readable event message, for example 'isolate start time: 96.67 ms (user time: 6.13 ms)'

urn.description: Optional URN for cross-referencing with observability artifacts, present on limit events

ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the event occurred
